title: astragalus australis var. aboriginorum
intro: Astragalus australis var. aboriginorum, also known as Aboriginorum milkvetch or Aboriginorum subalpine milkvetch, is a variety of the Astragalus australis plant that belongs to the legume family. It is a perennial herb native to Australia and is traditionally used in herbal medicine. The plant is valued for its adaptogenic and immune-boosting properties, which are attributed to its high content of flavonoids, saponins, and other bioactive compounds. In herbal preparations, the roots and leaves of Astragalus australis var. aboriginorum are often used in teas, tinctures, and infusions to support the health of the respiratory, digestive, and cardiovascular systems, and to promote overall well-being.
